ability.barbarian.recruit-blood-purge.name: Blood Purge
ability.barbarian.recruit-blood-purge.rulesText:
Barbarian Ability — Recruit
Reaction • Status

When a Poison status card would be placed in this character’s Status Row, prevent that card from being placed.

Remove all Poison status cards from this character’s Status Row. Then move Blood Purge from its equipped Skill Card into this character’s Status Row.

While Blood Purge is active, Poison status cards cannot be placed on this character.

At the end of combat, return Blood Purge to its equipped Skill Card.

source.sourceSection: Recruit Barbarian Ability Cards > Blood Purge
source.notes: Blood Purge is a reaction that prevents the triggering Poison card, removes any Poison cards already affecting its owner, and occupies the owner’s Status Row as temporary poison immunity until combat ends.

ability.barbarian.recruit-bloodied-defia.name: Bloodied Defiance
ability.barbarian.recruit-bloodied-defia.rulesText:
Barbarian Ability — Recruit
Passive
While this character is Raging and has current HP equal to or lower than half its maximum HP, this character gains +1 Defense.

source.sourceSection: Recruit Barbarian Ability Cards > Bloodied Defiance
source.notes: Bloodied Defiance is a Barbarian-only passive. While its owner is Raging and at or below half maximum HP, it grants +1 Defense. This intentionally offsets Rage's -1 Defense penalty, returning the owner to their normal Defense value rather than increasing Defense above baseline.

ability.barbarian.recruit-furious-retali.name: Furious Retaliation
ability.barbarian.recruit-furious-retali.rulesText:
Barbarian Ability — Recruit
Reaction
Trigger: While this character is Raging, an enemy melee attack deals at least 1 HP damage to this character.
Deal 1 Physical Damage to that enemy. Move Furious Retaliation to this character's Status Row.
Return Furious Retaliation to its equipped Skill Card at the end of the round.
This damage is not a weapon attack and does not count as damage dealt during this character's shared activation.

source.sourceSection: Recruit Barbarian Ability Cards > Furious Retaliation
source.notes: Furious Retaliation is a Barbarian-only reaction. It can trigger only while the owner is Raging and actually takes at least 1 HP damage from an enemy melee attack. Its retaliation damage is not a weapon attack, does not generate Heat, and does not count toward Rage maintenance.

ability.barbarian.recruit-rage.name: Rage
ability.barbarian.recruit-rage.rulesText:
Barbarian Ability — Recruit
Action • Status • Rage

Use this character’s shared activation. Move this card from its equipped Skill Card into this character’s Status Row.

While Rage is active:
• This character’s melee weapon attacks deal +1 damage.
• This character has -1 Defense.
• This character cannot cast spells.
• This character may still use non-spell abilities, reactions, items, and normal weapon attacks.

At the end of each later shared activation belonging to this character, check whether this character personally dealt 1 or more HP damage to an enemy during that activation.
• If they did, Rage remains active.
• If they did not, return Rage to its equipped Skill Card.
• If the shared activation was entirely lost because this character was Stunned, do not check Rage. Rage remains active.

At the end of combat, return Rage to its equipped Skill Card.

source.notes: Rage uses the owner’s shared activation to begin, occupies the owner’s Status Row while active, and returns to the exact Skill Card it came from when it ends. Count 1 is the provisional core-alpha supply.

ability.barbarian.recruit-reckless-strik.name: Reckless Strike
ability.barbarian.recruit-reckless-strik.rulesText:
Barbarian Ability — Recruit
Action • Melee • Physical

Use this character’s shared activation. Make a melee weapon attack with +1 damage.

After the attack resolves, this character gains an additional 1 Heat in addition to any Heat gained from dealing damage under the core combat rules.

Requires an equipped weapon with the Melee range tag.

source.sourceSection: Recruit Barbarian Ability Cards > Reckless Strike
source.notes: Reckless Strike must use a Melee-tagged equipped weapon. The +1 Heat in this card is additional to Heat normally generated when the attack deals HP damage.

ability.barbarian.recruit-unstoppable.name: Unstoppable
ability.barbarian.recruit-unstoppable.rulesText:
Barbarian Ability — Recruit
Reaction
Trigger: An enemy effect would apply Stunned, Slowed, or Restrained to this character.
Prevent that status from being applied. Move Unstoppable to this character's Status Row.
Return Unstoppable to its equipped Skill Card at the end of combat.

source.notes: Unstoppable is a Barbarian-only reaction that prevents one incoming application of Stunned, Slowed, or Restrained. It does not remove a status already in play. After resolving, it remains in the owner's Status Row until combat ends.

ability.warrior.recruit-blind-fighting.name: Blind Fighting
ability.warrior.recruit-blind-fighting.rulesText:
Warrior Ability — Recruit
Action
Use only while this character is Blinded.
Spend this character's shared activation: Make one standard melee weapon attack. Ignore the Blinded status only for this attack.

source.sourceSection: Recruit Warrior Ability Cards > Blind Fighting
source.notes: Blind Fighting is a general Warrior ability, not a Barbarian-only ability. It may be equipped beneath any Skill Card with the warrior tag. It remains equipped beneath that Skill Card after use and does not enter the Status Row.

ability.warrior.recruit-brace.name: Brace
ability.warrior.recruit-brace.rulesText:
Warrior Ability — Recruit
Action
Spend this character's shared activation: Move Brace to this character's Status Row. While Brace is in this character's Status Row, this character gains +1 Defense.
Return Brace to its equipped Skill Card at the end of the next enemy card turn.

source.notes: Brace is a general Warrior action. It trades the owner's shared activation for temporary Defense. While in the owner's Status Row, the card is unavailable for reuse. The applySelfModifierWhileInStatusRow effect and endOfNextEnemyCardTurn return timing require engine support.

ability.warrior.recruit-challenge.name: Challenge
ability.warrior.recruit-challenge.rulesText:
Warrior Ability — Recruit
Action
Spend this character's shared activation: This character gains 2 Heat.

source.notes: Challenge is a general Warrior action for intentionally drawing enemy targeting through the existing Heat system. It grants no damage or Defense bonus. The modifyHeat effect requires engine support if not already present.

ability.warrior.recruit-cleave.name: Cleave
ability.warrior.recruit-cleave.rulesText:
Warrior Ability — Recruit
Action
Requires a melee weapon with the Sword or Axe tag that requires 2 Hands.
Spend this character's shared activation: Make one standard melee weapon attack.
If that attack defeats its target, choose a different enemy in the defeated enemy's row. Deal 1 Physical Damage to the chosen enemy.

source.notes: Cleave is a general Warrior weapon-technique ability. It may be equipped beneath any Skill Card with the warrior tag. The initial attack is a standard melee weapon attack. The 1 Physical Damage follow-up is not a weapon attack, cannot receive weapon-attack bonuses, does not generate Heat, and does not count as damage dealt during the owner's shared activation.

ability.warrior.recruit-covering-guard.name: Covering Guard
ability.warrior.recruit-covering-guard.rulesText:
Warrior Ability — Recruit
Action
Choose another friendly entity in this character's row. Move Covering Guard to that entity's Status Row. While Covering Guard is there, that entity gains +1 Defense.
Return Covering Guard to its owner's equipped Skill Card at the end of the next enemy card turn.

source.sourceSection: Recruit Warrior Ability Cards > Covering Guard
source.notes: Covering Guard is proactive ally protection. The card is placed in the protected entity's Status Row so the bonus is visible where it applies, but it returns to the Warrior owner's equipped Skill Card. Target-status-row placement and owner-return behavior require engine support.

ability.warrior.recruit-interpose.name: Interpose
ability.warrior.recruit-interpose.rulesText:
Warrior Ability — Recruit
Reaction
Trigger: An enemy declares a single-target attack against another friendly entity in this character's row.
Change that attack's target to this character. Move Interpose to this character's Status Row.
Return Interpose to its equipped Skill Card at the end of the round.

source.notes: Interpose is a general Warrior reaction that may be equipped beneath any Skill Card with the warrior tag. It does not spend the owner's shared activation. After resolving, it moves to the owner's Status Row and cannot be used again until it returns to its equipped Skill Card at the end of the round. The redirected attack continues normally against the ability owner without causing the enemy to choose a new target.

ability.warrior.recruit-parry.name: Parry
ability.warrior.recruit-parry.rulesText:
Warrior Ability — Recruit
Reaction
Trigger: This character would take Physical damage from a melee attack.
Reduce that damage by 1. Move Parry to this character's Status Row.
Return Parry to its equipped Skill Card at the end of the round.

source.notes: Parry is a personal defensive reaction. It applies only to Physical damage from a melee attack, does not spend the owner's shared activation, and is unavailable again after resolving until the end of the round. The reactive damage-reduction trigger and effect require engine support.

ability.warrior.recruit-shake-it-off.name: Shake It Off
ability.warrior.recruit-shake-it-off.rulesText:
Warrior Ability — Recruit
Action
Spend this character's shared activation: Discard one negative status card from this character with the Sensory, Physical, or Movement tag.

source.sourceSection: Recruit Warrior Ability Cards > Shake It Off
source.notes: Shake It Off deliberately removes only a limited set of negative status categories. It does not remove curse, mental, magical, poison, or disease statuses unless they also carry one of the allowed tags. This requires formal status tags and a discardNegativeStatus effect.

ability.warrior.recruit-shield-bash.name: Shield Bash
ability.warrior.recruit-shield-bash.rulesText:
Warrior Ability — Recruit
Action
Requires an equipped shield.
Spend this character's shared activation: Deal 1 Physical damage to an enemy in this character's melee range. If this deals at least 1 HP damage, that enemy cannot use Reactions until the end of the round.

source.sourceSection: Recruit Warrior Ability Cards > Shield Bash
source.notes: Shield Bash is a shield-only tactical control action, not a standard weapon attack. It is intentionally weaker than most normal weapon attacks in raw damage. It needs a Reaction Disabled negative status card or equivalent status implementation before it can function.

ability.warrior.recruit-stand-firm.name: Stand Firm
ability.warrior.recruit-stand-firm.rulesText:
Warrior Ability — Recruit
Passive
This character cannot be moved to another row by an enemy effect unless that effect specifically says it ignores Stand Firm.

source.sourceSection: Recruit Warrior Ability Cards > Stand Firm
source.notes: Stand Firm is a general Warrior passive intended for use once enemies, traps, or dungeon events can force entities between rows. It occupies an ability slot but does not consume an activation. Forced row-movement prevention requires engine support.

character.cleric.name: Cleric
character.cleric.rulesText:
Player Character — Divine

Maximum HP: 14
Suggested First Placement: Player Back Row

Class Trait — Sacred Relief
Once per Encounter Round, when the Cleric restores 1 or more HP to another Friendly Entity, remove 1 Heat from that entity.

Native Skill Domains
Restoration; Divine Intervention; Exorcism.

source.notes: HP, Sacred Relief, native domains, recommended starter cards, and starting defense are migrated from the starter roster. Referenced equipment and spells will be added in later bulk packs.

character.druid.name: Druid
character.druid.rulesText:
Player Character — Primal

Maximum HP: 14
Suggested First Placement: Player Back Row

Class Trait — Wild Pathfinder
While traveling on foot, whenever the Druid reveals 1 or more map tiles, reveal 1 additional adjacent face-down tile. This cannot reveal a tile beyond the map boundary.

Native Skill Domains
Biomancy; Wildcalling; Primalism.

source.notes: HP, Wild Pathfinder, native domains, recommended starter cards, and starting defense are migrated from the starter roster. Wild Pathfinder is retained even though it is outside the initial dungeon-only smoke test.

character.mage.name: Mage
character.mage.rulesText:
Player Character — Arcane

Maximum HP: 12
Suggested First Placement: Player Back Row

Class Trait — Spellweaver
The first damaging Spell the Mage casts during its own normal action in each Encounter Round deals +1 additional damage.

Native Skill Domains
Elementalism; Arcana; Necromancy.

source.notes: This replacement record updates the starter-loadout IDs to the canonical Mage starter card IDs included in this pack. Several referenced Tactical Reserve cards are expected to already exist in the catalog.

character.rogue.name: Rogue
character.rogue.rulesText:
Player Character — Martial

Maximum HP: 18
Suggested First Placement: Player Front Row

Class Trait — Shadow Step
Once per Encounter Round, immediately before or after the Rogue uses its own normal action, move the Rogue to an empty Player Front Row or Player Back Row slot. This movement does not spend an action.

Native Skill Domains
Thief; Skirmisher; Shadowcraft.

source.notes: HP, Shadow Step, native domains, starter equipment, Tactical Reserve, and baseline defense are migrated from the starter roster. The source called the initial Thief and Skirmisher ranks Novice; this catalog record intentionally uses the finalized Rogue rank name Runner.

character.warrior.name: Warrior
character.warrior.rulesText:
Player Character — Martial

Maximum HP: 20
Suggested First Placement: Player Front Row

Class Trait — Weapon Mastery
This character's melee weapon attacks ignore 1 Defense.

Native Skill Domains
Barbarian; Vanguard; Marksman.

source.notes: Maximum HP, class trait, native skill domains, starting equipment, starter skills, Tactical Reserve cards, and starting Defense are migrated from the first-playtest character roster. Several referenced catalog records have not yet been created and are intentionally retained as future references.

dungeon.goblin-warrens.goblin-alarm.name: Goblin Alarm
dungeon.goblin-warrens.goblin-alarm.rulesText:
Event

The active character gains 2 Heat.

Reveal the bottom 2 cards of the Dungeon Deck.

Add the first revealed Goblin Enemy card to an open Enemy Formation slot.

Shuffle every other revealed card back into the Dungeon Deck.

source.sourceSection: Goblin Warrens / Scrap Fortress > Goblin Alarm
source.notes: Rules text migrated from the current Goblin Warrens source. Count 2 is a provisional first-playtest density. The source text does not explicitly state the event card's own post-resolution destination, so dungeonDiscardPile is modeled as the current one-shot dungeon-event default and should be confirmed during deck-rule review.

dungeon.goblin-warrens.goblin-stolen-goo.name: Stolen Goods Stash
dungeon.goblin-warrens.goblin-stolen-goo.rulesText:
Event

 Draw one card from the loot deck and add it to the dungeon loot stash.

source.sourceSection: Goblin Warrens / Scrap Fortress > Goblin Alarm
source.notes: Rules text migrated from the current Goblin Warrens source. Count 2 is a provisional first-playtest density. The source text does not explicitly state the event card's own post-resolution destination, so dungeonDiscardPile is modeled as the current one-shot dungeon-event default and should be confirmed during deck-rule review.

dungeon.goblin-warrens.mess.name: Goblin Mess Hall
dungeon.goblin-warrens.mess.rulesText:
Area

 All player characters gain 1 HP.

source.notes: Rules text migrated from the current Goblin Warrens dungeon-card source. Count 2 is a provisional first-playtest deck-density decision and can be tuned after initial dungeon runs.

dungeon.goblin-warrens.tripwire.name: Tripwire
dungeon.goblin-warrens.tripwire.rulesText:
Trap

A player-controlled character in the active character's current row with an equipped Rogue skill may disarm Tripwire. If so, discard Tripwire.

Otherwise, the active character takes 1 Physical damage and gains 2 Heat.

Discard Tripwire.

source.notes: Rules text migrated from the current Goblin Warrens dungeon-card source. Count 2 is a provisional first-playtest deck-density decision and can be tuned after initial dungeon runs.

enemy.goblin-archer.name: Goblin Archer
enemy.goblin-archer.rulesText:
Crude Shortbow
Deal 1 physical damage, ranged.

Loot: 1 Coin.

source.notes: I created this

enemy.goblin-bombardier.name: Goblin Bombardier
enemy.goblin-bombardier.rulesText:
Firepot
Deal 2 fire damage, ranged. Each other legal friendly entity takes 1 fire damage.

Loot: 3 Coin.

source.notes: Goblin Warrens playtest enemy. Count 1 represents one deck instance. The splash-damage rider is intentionally represented in rulesText until a confirmed structured multi-target action field is available.

enemy.goblin-bully.name: Goblin Bully
enemy.goblin-bully.rulesText:
Club Smash
Deal 3 physical damage, melee.

Loot: 2 Coin.

source.notes: Goblin Warrens playtest enemy. Count 2 represents two deck instances; this file is the single shared definition.

enemy.goblin-cutthroat.name: Goblin Cutthroat
enemy.goblin-cutthroat.rulesText:
Dirty Strike
Deal 2 physical damage, melee. The target gains 1 Poison status card.

Loot: 2 Coin.

source.notes: Goblin Warrens playtest enemy. Count 2 represents two deck instances; this file is the single shared definition. The Poison rider is intentionally represented in rulesText until a confirmed structured status-effect action field is available.

enemy.goblin-hexer.name: Goblin Hexer
enemy.goblin-hexer.rulesText:
Hex Bolt
Deal 1 arcane damage, ranged. The target gains 1 Poison status card.

Loot: 3 Coin.

source.notes: Goblin Warrens playtest enemy. Count 1 represents one deck instance. The Poison rider is intentionally represented in rulesText until a confirmed structured status-effect action field is available.

enemy.goblin-raider.name: Goblin Raider
enemy.goblin-raider.rulesText:
Rusty Blade
Deal 2 physical damage, melee.

Loot: 1 Coin.

source.notes: HP 6, Rusty Blade for 2 physical melee damage, and 1 Coin loot are migrated from the current Goblin enemy source. Defense 0, Enemy Front Row placement, and count 4 are provisional catalog decisions for the first playtest.

enemy.goblin-skirmisher.name: Goblin Skirmisher
enemy.goblin-skirmisher.rulesText:
Jagged Slash
Deal 1 physical damage, melee.

Loot: 1 Coin.

source.notes: Goblin Warrens playtest enemy. Count 3 represents three deck instances; this file is the single shared definition.

enemy.goblin-slinger.name: Goblin Slinger
enemy.goblin-slinger.rulesText:
Sling Stone
Deal 1 physical damage, ranged.

Loot: 1 Coin.

source.notes: Goblin Warrens playtest enemy. Count 3 represents three deck instances; this file is the single shared definition.

enemy.goblin-sneak.name: Goblin Sneak
enemy.goblin-sneak.rulesText:
Backstab
Deal 2 physical damage, melee, to the legal friendly entity with the lowest current HP. Break ties by highest Heat.

Loot: 1 Coin.

source.notes: Goblin Warrens playtest enemy. Count 2 represents two deck instances; this file is the single shared definition. The special target-selection instruction is kept in player-facing rules text because the confirmed action schema only provides defaultHighestHeatLegalFriendlyEntity.

enemy.goblin-spearman.name: Goblin Spearman
enemy.goblin-spearman.rulesText:
Spear Thrust
Deal 2 physical damage, melee.

Loot: 1 Coin.

source.notes: Goblin Warrens playtest enemy. Count 3 represents three deck instances; this file is the single shared definition.

enemy.goblin-worg-rider.name: Goblin Worg Rider
enemy.goblin-worg-rider.rulesText:
Trampling Charge
Deal 3 physical damage, melee. If the target has at least 1 Poison status card, it gains 1 Stun status card.

Loot: 3 Coin.

source.notes: Goblin Warrens playtest enemy. Count 1 represents one deck instance. The conditional Stun rider is intentionally represented in rulesText until a confirmed structured status-effect action field is available.

enemy.goblin-worg.name: Goblin Worg
enemy.goblin-worg.rulesText:
Rending Bite
Deal 2 physical damage, melee. If the target has at least 1 Poison status card, deal 1 additional physical damage.

Loot: 1 Coin.

source.notes: Goblin Warrens playtest enemy. Count 2 represents two deck instances; this file is the single shared definition. The conditional damage rider is intentionally represented in rulesText until a confirmed structured conditional-effect action field is available.

equipment.body.apprentice-robes.name: Apprentice Robes
equipment.body.apprentice-robes.rulesText:
Body Equipment — Clothing
Clothing • Robe
+0 Defense

source.notes: Canonical spelling follows the equipment catalog: Apprentice Robes, plural. The starter roster uses the singular phrase Apprentice Robe.

equipment.body.chainmail.name: Chainmail
equipment.body.chainmail.rulesText:
Body Equipment — Armor
Armor • Medium Armor • Chain
+3 Defense
Requires Medium Armor Proficiency

source.notes: Medium body armor. Chainmail follows the established armor progression: Clothing +0, Leather +1, Scale Mail or Brigandine +2, Chainmail +3, and Full Plate +4 Defense.

equipment.body.full-plate.name: Full Plate
equipment.body.full-plate.rulesText:
Body Equipment — Armor
Armor • Heavy Armor • Plate
+4 Defense
Requires Heavy Armor Proficiency

source.notes: Top-tier standard body armor. Full Plate provides +4 Defense and requires Heavy Armor Proficiency.

equipment.body.half-plate.name: Half-Plate
equipment.body.half-plate.rulesText:
Body Equipment — Armor
Armor • Heavy Armor • Half-Plate
+3 Defense
Requires Heavy Armor Proficiency

source.notes: High-tier body armor. Half-Plate provides +3 Defense and is intended to sit immediately below Full Plate at +4 Defense.

equipment.body.leather-armor.name: Leather Armor
equipment.body.leather-armor.rulesText:
Body Equipment — Armor
Armor • Light Armor • Leather
+1 Defense

source.notes: Starter light body armor. Provides +1 Defense and carries the Light Armor and Leather subtype tags for future proficiency or ability requirements.

equipment.feet.surefoot-boots.name: Surefoot Boots
equipment.feet.surefoot-boots.rulesText:
Feet Equipment
Clothing • Boots
Surefooted: Once per combat, ignore one effect that would force you to change rows.

traits: Surefooted: Once per combat, ignore one effect that would force you to change rows.

source.notes: The once-per-combat trait should use the established equipment-use tracking method.

equipment.feet.travel-boots.name: Travel Boots
equipment.feet.travel-boots.rulesText:
Feet Equipment
Clothing • Boots
No mechanical effect.

source.notes: Starter footwear with no mechanical effect.

equipment.hands.weapon-grip-gloves.name: Weapon Grip Gloves
equipment.hands.weapon-grip-gloves.rulesText:
Handwear
Clothing • Gloves
Weapon Grip: Once per combat, when you make a melee weapon attack, add +1 Physical Damage.

traits: Weapon Grip: Once per combat, when you make a melee weapon attack, add +1 Physical Damage.

source.sourceSection: Handwear Equipment Cards > Weapon Grip Gloves
source.notes: The once-per-combat trait should use the established equipment-use tracking method.

equipment.hands.work-gloves.name: Work Gloves
equipment.hands.work-gloves.rulesText:
Handwear
Clothing • Gloves
No mechanical effect.

source.notes: Starter handwear with no mechanical effect.

equipment.neck.guardian-pendant.name: Guardian Pendant
equipment.neck.guardian-pendant.rulesText:
Neck Equipment
Accessory • Pendant
Guardian Ward: Once per combat, when you would take damage, reduce that damage by 1, to a minimum of 0.

traits: Guardian Ward: Once per combat, when you would take damage, reduce that damage by 1, to a minimum of 0.

source.notes: This is damage reduction, not a permanent Defense bonus. The once-per-combat trait should use the established equipment-use tracking method.

equipment.neck.simple-pendant.name: Simple Pendant
equipment.neck.simple-pendant.rulesText:
Neck Equipment
Accessory • Pendant
No mechanical effect.

source.notes: Starter neck item with no mechanical effect.

equipment.focus.apprentices-wand.name: Apprentice's Wand
equipment.focus.apprentices-wand.rulesText:
Off-Hand Focus
Arcane • Wand

No weapon attack.
This card may satisfy a card requirement for an Arcane Focus.

source.notes: The starter roster defines this as a held Arcane Focus with no weapon attack. Economy fields are provisional because the starter roster does not assign an EPR or market value.

equipment.offhand.buckler.name: Buckler
equipment.offhand.buckler.rulesText:
Off-Hand Equipment — Shield
Shield • Buckler
+1 Defense

source.notes: Starter shield. Shields occupy the off-hand slot. Defense reduces Physical damage only.

equipment.offhand.crystal-focus.name: Crystal Focus
equipment.offhand.crystal-focus.rulesText:
Off-Hand Equipment — Focus
Focus • Crystal • Arcane
Crystal Channeling: Once per combat, when you resolve an Arcana spell that deals damage, add +1 damage.

traits: Crystal Channeling: Once per combat, when you resolve an Arcana spell that deals damage, add +1 damage.

source.notes: The once-per-combat trait should use the established equipment-use tracking method.

equipment.offhand.ember-focus.name: Ember Focus
equipment.offhand.ember-focus.rulesText:
Off-Hand Equipment — Focus
Focus • Ember • Elementalism
Ember Channeling: Once per combat, when you resolve an Elementalism spell that deals Fire damage, add +1 Fire damage.

traits: Ember Channeling: Once per combat, when you resolve an Elementalism spell that deals Fire damage, add +1 Fire damage.

source.notes: The once-per-combat trait should use the established equipment-use tracking method.

equipment.offhand.kite-shield.name: Kite Shield
equipment.offhand.kite-shield.rulesText:
Off-Hand Equipment — Shield
Shield • Kite Shield
+2 Defense

source.notes: Starter shield. Shields occupy the off-hand slot. Defense reduces Physical damage only.

equipment.offhand.square-shield.name: Square Shield
equipment.offhand.square-shield.rulesText:
Off-Hand Equipment — Shield
Shield • Square Shield
+2 Defense

source.notes: Starter shield. Shields occupy the off-hand slot. Defense reduces Physical damage only.

equipment.offhand.sunstone-focus.name: Sunstone Focus
equipment.offhand.sunstone-focus.rulesText:
Off-Hand Equipment — Focus
Focus • Sunstone • Divine
Sunstone Channeling: Once per combat, when you resolve a Divine spell that restores HP, restore +1 additional HP.

traits: Sunstone Channeling: Once per combat, when you resolve a Divine spell that restores HP, restore +1 additional HP.

source.notes: The once-per-combat trait should use the established equipment-use tracking method.

equipment.ring.emberward-ring.name: Emberward Ring
equipment.ring.emberward-ring.rulesText:
Ring Equipment
Accessory • Ring • Fire
Emberward: Reduce Fire damage you take by 1, to a minimum of 0.

traits: Emberward: Reduce Fire damage you take by 1, to a minimum of 0.

source.notes: This is damage reduction, not a permanent Defense bonus.

equipment.ring.plain-ring.name: Plain Ring
equipment.ring.plain-ring.rulesText:
Ring Equipment
Accessory • Ring
No mechanical effect.

source.notes: Starter ring with no mechanical effect. The current starter catalog uses one ring slot.

equipment.ring.venomward-ring.name: Venomward Ring
equipment.ring.venomward-ring.rulesText:
Ring Equipment
Accessory • Ring • Poison
Venomward: Once per combat, when you would gain a Poison status card, prevent that card from being placed.

traits: Venomward: Once per combat, when you would gain a Poison status card, prevent that card from being placed.

source.notes: The once-per-combat trait should use the established equipment-use tracking method.

equipment.weapon.arming-sword.name: Arming Sword
equipment.weapon.arming-sword.rulesText:
Weapon
Melee • Blade • Sword
2 Physical Damage
Requires Blade proficiency.
Hands Required: 1 Hand.

source.notes: The printed weapon values are migrated from the starter equipment catalog. Count 2 is a provisional first-playtest supply decision.

equipment.weapon.battleaxe.name: Battleaxe
equipment.weapon.battleaxe.rulesText:
Weapon
Melee • Axe • Battleaxe
3 Physical Damage
Requires Axe proficiency.
Hands Required: 2 Hands.

source.notes: Starter weapon catalog entry. Weapon tags support range and subtype eligibility checks.

equipment.weapon.hand-axe.name: Hand Axe
equipment.weapon.hand-axe.rulesText:
Weapon
Melee • Axe • Hand-Axe
2 Physical Damage
Requires Axe proficiency.
Hands Required: 1 Hand.

source.notes: Starter weapon catalog entry. Weapon tags support range and subtype eligibility checks.

equipment.weapon.iron-spear.name: Iron Spear
equipment.weapon.iron-spear.rulesText:
Weapon
Melee • Spear
2 Physical Damage
Requires Spear proficiency.
Hands Required: 2 Hands.

source.notes: Starter weapon catalog entry. Weapon tags support range and subtype eligibility checks.

equipment.weapon.light-crossbow.name: Light Crossbow
equipment.weapon.light-crossbow.rulesText:
Weapon
Ranged • Crossbow • Light-Crossbow
2 Physical Damage
Requires Crossbow proficiency.
Hands Required: 2 Hands.

source.notes: Starter weapon catalog entry. Weapon tags support range and subtype eligibility checks.

Traits: Arcane Implement: You may treat this weapon as an Arcane Focus.

hireling.roadside-sellsword.name: Roadside Sellsword
hireling.roadside-sellsword.rulesText:
Hireling — Mercenary — Autonomous Ally — Market Only

Minimum Settlement: Village
Currency Price: 14
Preferred Row: Player Front Row
HP: 6
Defense: 1

Priority:
1. Sword Cut
If this ally is in the Player Front Row, deal 2 Physical damage to the enemy in the opposing Front Row with the highest printed Attack. Gain 1 Heat.

2. Advance
If this ally is in the Player Back Row and an empty Player Front Row slot exists, move to the leftmost empty Player Front Row slot.

Defeat: Put this card into the Loot Discard Pile.

source.notes: Count 1 is intentional. This is a persistent named market card, not a disposable enemy supply. If dismissed or defeated, it enters the Loot Discard Pile and can become available again when the Loot Deck is reshuffled.

skill.arcana.novice.name: Novice Arcana
skill.arcana.novice.rulesText:
Arcana Skill — Novice

Attachment Bays
• Subdomain Bay: 1 Novice Arcana spell or ability.
• Class Bay: 1 Novice Mage spell or ability.

An attached card must require Novice rank or lower.

Passive — Arcane Foundation
No passive effect is finalized yet. This card is active for early Mage tabletop testing and should receive a finalized printed passive before production.

source.sourceSection: Mage Character Card > Starting Skills and Attached Spells
source.notes: Temporary app-testing configuration: slotCount is deliberately high so multiple Mage and Arcana spell or ability cards can be attached without a UI limit. The printed tabletop capacity remains one Arcana Subdomain Bay and one Mage Class Bay on this Novice skill card. Replace the temporary attachment limit with typed-bay enforcement when that UI and validation system is implemented.

skill.barbarian.recruit-feral-instinct.name: Feral Instinct
skill.barbarian.recruit-feral-instinct.rulesText:
Barbarian Skill — Recruit

Attachment Bays:
Top — Subdomain Ability / Spell: 1
Bottom — Class Ability / Spell: 1

Tabletop Rules:
• The top bay may hold one Recruit Barbarian Ability or Spell.
• The bottom bay may hold one Recruit Warrior Ability or Spell.

Passive — Rising Fury
The first time this character deals 1 or more HP damage to an enemy during each of their shared activations, gain 1 additional Heat.

source.sourceSection: Barbarian Skill Cards > Recruit: Feral Instinct
source.notes: The app treats this Skill Card as a flexible attachment host for prototype testing. The app-level capacity is intentionally high and does not enforce discipline, rank, Class-bay, or Subdomain-bay legality. Players enforce the printed tabletop rules when building a legal character.

skill.elementalism.novice.name: Novice Elementalism
skill.elementalism.novice.rulesText:
Elementalism Skill — Novice

Attachment Bays
• Subdomain Bay: 1 Novice Elementalism spell or ability.
• Class Bay: 1 Novice Mage spell or ability.

An attached card must require Novice rank or lower.

Passive — Attuned Casting
Whenever one of your Fire, Ice, Lightning, or Earth spells deals damage to a target, the first 1 damage dealt to that target by that spell ignores Defense.

source.notes: Temporary app-testing configuration: slotCount is deliberately high so multiple Mage and Elementalism spell or ability cards can be attached without a UI limit. The printed tabletop capacity remains one Elementalism Subdomain Bay and one Mage Class Bay on this Novice skill card. Replace the temporary attachment limit with typed-bay enforcement when that UI and validation system is implemented. Attuned Casting is migrated from the Elementalism card file.

spell.arcana.novice-arcane-ward.name: Arcane Ward
spell.arcana.novice-arcane-ward.rulesText:
Arcana Spell — Novice
Action • Persistent Positive Effect

Use this character's shared activation. Move this card from its equipped Skill Card into the Mage's Status Row.

The Mage gains +1 Defense until the start of the Mage's next shared activation.

When this effect ends, return this card to Novice Arcana.

source.sourceSection: Mage Required Starter Companion Card Text > Arcane Ward
source.notes: Text and duration are migrated from the Mage starter roster. The generic Status Row UI can move this card to the Mage's Status Row because activation.moveTo is ownerStatusRow.

spell.elementalism.novice-firebolt.name: Firebolt
spell.elementalism.novice-firebolt.rulesText:
Elementalism Spell — Novice
Action • Spell Attack

Deal 2 Fire damage to 1 enemy in either opposing row.
Gain 1 Heat.

source.sourceSection: Mage Required Starter Companion Card Text; Novice Elementalism Spells
source.notes: The Mage starter roster specifies 2 Fire damage and gain 1 Heat. The older Elementalism file defines Fire Bolt as 1 Fire damage with no Heat. This pack intentionally follows the newer starter-roster version for first-playtest Mage setup; reconcile the source conflict before print finalization.

status.negative.poisoned.name: Poisoned
status.negative.poisoned.rulesText:
Negative Status — Poison

At the start of the affected entity's next applicable activation, that entity takes 1 Poison damage. Then discard this card.

Poisoned stacks. Resolve each Poisoned card separately.

source.notes: Count 8 is a provisional first-playtest Status Deck supply. Each Poisoned card is a separate stackable status card and resolves independently.

status.negative.stunned.name: Stunned
status.negative.stunned.rulesText:
Negative Status — Stun

At the start of the affected entity's next applicable activation, that entity loses its next normal activation. Then discard this card.

Player Character: That character's group loses its shared activation and cannot activate a Controlled Ally.
Controlled Ally: This ally cannot be selected for its owner's shared activation. The owner may still act or select another eligible Controlled Ally.
Autonomous Ally: This ally loses only its independent activation.

Stun does not prevent passive effects, statuses, reactions, or triggered abilities unless they require the Stunned entity to take an action.

exception.rule: An effect remains allowed only when it does not require the Stunned entity to take an action.

source.sourceSection: 7.9.1 Negative Status Effects; 7.9.3 Status Timing; 7.9.4 Stun and Activations
source.notes: Count 4 is a provisional first-playtest Status Deck supply. Stunned does not stack. It is intentionally modeled by affected entity type because player characters, Controlled Allies, Autonomous Allies, enemies, and bosses have different activation structures.

tactical-reserve.antidote.name: Antidote
tactical-reserve.antidote.rulesText:
Tactical Reserve — Action

Remove 1 Poisoned status card from 1 Friendly Entity.

After resolving, shuffle this card into the Loot Deck.

source.sourceSection: Shared Starting Tactical Reserve Cards > Antidote
source.notes: Count 8 covers the five starter-character references and leaves three additional copies for early Loot Deck use.

tactical-reserve.bandage.name: Bandage
tactical-reserve.bandage.rulesText:
Tactical Reserve — Action

Remove Bleeding from 1 Friendly Entity.
If that entity has no Bleeding card, restore 1 HP instead.

After resolving, shuffle this card into the Loot Deck.

source.sourceSection: Shared Starting Tactical Reserve Cards > Bandage
source.notes: Count 4 is a provisional module supply. The Bleeding status card has not yet been converted to the catalog.

tactical-reserve.barkskin-salve.name: Barkskin Salve
tactical-reserve.barkskin-salve.rulesText:
Tactical Reserve — Action — Persistent Positive Effect

Place this card in 1 Friendly Entity's Status Row. That entity gains +1 Defense until the start of its next activation.

When this effect ends, shuffle this card into the Loot Deck.

source.sourceSection: Shared Starting Tactical Reserve Cards > Barkskin Salve
source.notes: Count 4 is a provisional module supply. This is a Tactical Reserve card that occupies the target's Status Row while active.

tactical-reserve.healing-potion.name: Healing Potion
tactical-reserve.healing-potion.rulesText: Restore 3 HP to one friendly entity. After resolving, shuffle this card into the Loot Deck.

source.notes: Bootstrap sample only. Confirm the final Healing Potion wording and healing amount against the approved Tactical Reserve card design before treating this record as print-ready.

tactical-reserve.holy-water.name: Holy Water
tactical-reserve.holy-water.rulesText:
Tactical Reserve — Action

Deal 2 Force damage to 1 enemy in either opposing row. If that enemy has the Undead tag, deal +2 damage instead.

After resolving, shuffle this card into the Loot Deck.

source.sourceSection: Shared Starting Tactical Reserve Cards > Holy Water
source.notes: Count 4 is a provisional module supply.

tactical-reserve.scroll-of-force-dart.name: Scroll of Force Dart
tactical-reserve.scroll-of-force-dart.rulesText:
Tactical Reserve — Action

Deal 2 Force damage to 1 enemy in either opposing row. Gain 1 Heat.

After resolving, shuffle this card into the Loot Deck.

source.sourceSection: Shared Starting Tactical Reserve Cards > Scroll of Force Dart
source.notes: Count 4 is a provisional module supply.

tactical-reserve.smoke-bomb.name: Smoke Bomb
tactical-reserve.smoke-bomb.rulesText:
Tactical Reserve — Reaction

Use when the Rogue is chosen as the target of an enemy attack.
Prevent 2 damage from that attack. Then move the Rogue to an empty Player Back Row slot, if able.

After resolving, shuffle this card into the Loot Deck.

source.sourceSection: Shared Starting Tactical Reserve Cards > Smoke Bomb
source.notes: Count 4 is a provisional module supply. The printed trigger is Rogue-specific.

deck.goblin-warrens-catalog-smoke-test.name: Goblin Warrens Catalog Coverage Test
deck.goblin-warrens-catalog-smoke-test.rulesText: A Goblin Warrens dungeon deck for catalog coverage and early manual playtesting. This definition is the source of truth for all base Dungeon Deck cards. The selected scenario may separately inject Loot Deck cards during fresh-state setup.

knownLimitations: This deck is intended for catalog coverage and early manual playtesting, not final encounter balance.
This deck has no dedicated boss, objective, room, reward, or completion-condition cards yet.
Goblin Alarm can reveal cards from the bottom of the Dungeon Deck and deploy the first matching Goblin enemy it finds.
Scenario-level Loot Deck injection is deliberately not listed here because it belongs to the selected scenario.

source.notes: Generated from all active enemy.goblin-* definitions plus active Goblin Warrens Tripwire and Goblin Alarm definitions. Base deck total: 32.

scenario.solo-warrior-goblin-warrens-smo.name: Solo Warrior: Goblin Warrens Smoke Test
scenario.solo-warrior-goblin-warrens-smo.rulesText: A minimal manual playtest setup for validating scenario loading, card-instance creation, equipment, Skill Card attachments, Tactical Reserve placement, Dungeon Deck shuffling, manual drawing, enemy deployment, and Dungeon Discard behavior.

endCondition.notes: This is a catalog and tabletop smoke test, not a complete dungeon. Stop when the intended card, deck, row, counter, and discard interactions have been tested.

knownLimitations: This scenario does not yet include a full starting loadout.
This scenario does not yet include a boss, dungeon objective, or normal dungeon-completion condition.
This scenario intentionally begins with only one filled Tactical Reserve slot.
Combat, targeting, damage, Heat, and status resolution remain manually controlled during this early playtest stage.

source.notes: First scenario-definition test. It references only currently cataloged active card and deck definitions, and intentionally avoids the Warrior card's unconverted starter-loadout references.
